label: Bhadla Solar Park
sub: 2,700 MW — World's largest solar park (Rajasthan, India). Operational since 2020.

label: Hornsea 2 Offshore Wind
sub: 1,386 MW — World's largest offshore wind farm at construction. Located in the North Sea, UK. Won contract at £39.65/MWh in 2019.

label: Jiuquan Wind Power Base
sub: 20,000 MW planned — Gansu Province, China. Largest wind energy base in the world. Core of China's western renewable supergrid.

label: Noor Solar Complex
sub: 580 MW CSP + PV — Ouarzazate, Morocco. Africa's largest solar facility; integrates 3-hour molten salt thermal storage.

label: Hellisheidi Geothermal
sub: 303 MW electric / 400 MW heat — Iceland's largest power plant; powers Reykjavik. Site co-located with Climeworks carbon capture plant.

label: Climeworks Mammoth (DAC)
sub: 36,000 tCO₂/yr — World's largest direct air capture plant, opened May 2024. CO₂ mineralizes in basalt rock permanently.

label: Drax BECCS Pilot (UK)
sub: Yorkshire, UK. Drax Power Station is piloting bioenergy with carbon capture and storage (BECCS) — capturing CO₂ from biomass combustion. Planned for full operation in late 2020s.

label: Boundary Dam CCS
sub: Saskatchewan, Canada. World's first commercial post-combustion CCS on a coal power plant, operational 2014. Captures ~1 MtCO₂/yr when running at capacity.

label: Tesla Gigafactory 1
sub: Sparks, Nevada, USA. World's largest battery manufacturing facility; produces ~100 GWh/yr of Li-ion cells for Tesla vehicles and Powerwall storage.

label: VW PowerCo Gigafactory
sub: Salzgitter, Germany. Volkswagen's first in-house battery cell gigafactory, part of €20B plan for 6 European battery plants by 2030. Anchors Europe's battery supply chain transition.

label: EU Green Deal HQ (Brussels)
sub: European Commission headquarters. Origin of the European Green Deal (2019), EU Climate Law (2021), Fit for 55 package, and REPowerEU — the world's most comprehensive climate legislation.

label: COP21 — Paris Agreement
sub: Le Bourget, Paris, France. Site of COP21 (Dec 2015) where 195 nations adopted the Paris Agreement — the central framework for global climate action.

label: COP28 — Dubai Consensus
sub: Expo City Dubai, UAE. COP28 (Nov–Dec 2023) produced the first global pledge to 'transition away from fossil fuels' and commitments to triple renewables + double efficiency by 2030.

label: Lake Turkana Wind Farm
sub: 310 MW — Kenya. Africa's largest wind farm (310 turbines), supplying ~17% of Kenya's installed grid capacity. Reduces diesel generation, cutting both cost and emissions.

label: Amazon REDD+ Zone
sub: Amazonas State, Brazil. Core zone for forest protection under REDD+ and Brazil's Amazon Fund ($3.8B pledged by Norway, Germany, USA). Deforestation down 50% since 2021 peak.

label: Great Green Wall
sub: Dakar, Senegal. Anchor site of Africa's Great Green Wall — a 8,000 km belt of trees across the Sahel from Senegal to Djibouti. Aims to restore 100M ha of degraded land by 2030.

label: Three Gorges Dam
sub: 22,500 MW — Yichang, Hubei, China. World's largest hydropower plant; generates ~100 TWh/yr, displacing ~50 Mt CO₂ annually vs equivalent coal generation.

label: Kamuthi Solar Power Plant
sub: 648 MW — Tamil Nadu, India. Built in just 8 months in 2016; was the world's largest single-location solar plant at time of completion. Self-cleaning robotic panels.

label: El Romero Solar (Atacama)
sub: 246 MW — Atacama Desert, Chile. Chile leads Latin America in solar with some of the world's highest irradiation. Chile hit 30% renewable electricity by 2022.

label: Bonn Challenge Coordination
sub: Bonn, Germany. Origin of the Bonn Challenge (2011) — global goal to restore 350 million hectares of deforested land by 2030. Over 73 pledging nations and organizations have committed 210M+ ha.

label: COP30 — Belém Package
sub: Belém, Brazil. COP30 (November 2025) adopted the 'Belém Package' — 29 decisions including a Just Transition mechanism, commitment to triple adaptation finance by 2035, and Brazil's Tropical Forest Forever Facility (USD 125B blended-finance fund). First COP held in the Amazon.

label: EU 2040 Climate Law (90%)
sub: Brussels, Belgium. EU Regulation 2026/667 entered into force April 7, 2026 — the world's most ambitious legally binding medium-term climate target: 90% net GHG reduction by 2040 vs. 1990. Trajectory: −55% (2030) → −90% (2040) → net-zero (2050).

label: Santa Marta Fossil Fuel Conference
sub: Santa Marta, Colombia. Site of the First International Conference on Transitioning Away from Fossil Fuels (April 24–29, 2026), co-hosted by Colombia and the Netherlands. 60+ nations attended; Science Panel for Global Energy Transition (SPGET) launched here by Johan Rockström and Carlos Nobre. First summit dedicated to fossil fuel phase-out, operating outside UNFCCC framework.
